trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade under AGOA (African Growth and Opportunity Act), which grants Sudafrica preferential access to Stati Uniti markets. Sudafrica exports vehicles, minerals, precious metals, and fruits to the Stati Uniti; the Stati Uniti exports machinery, aircraft, chemicals, and electronics to Sudafrica. AGOA renewal is a persistent bilateral trade issue.
disputes: Sudafrica's land reform policies raise Stati Uniti investor concerns
AGOA renewal uncertainty and Sudafrica's trade diversification
Stati Uniti concerns over Sudafrica's BRI engagement and Russia arms trade

diplomatic.summary: Stati Uniti-Sudafrica relations have been significantly strained in recent years. Sudafrica's refusal to condemn Russia's invasion of Ucraina, its participation in joint naval exercises with Russia and Cina, its ICJ genocide case against Israele, and its BRICS deepening have all caused diplomatic friction with the Stati Uniti. The Stati Uniti has periodically threatened to review Sudafrica's AGOA status. Sudafrica maintains a principled non-aligned posture that increasingly diverges from Stati Uniti preferences.
recent_incidents: Sudafrica abstained on UN votes condemning Russia's Ucraina invasion
Sudafrica hosted Russia-Cina joint naval exercises (2022-2023)
Sudafrica filed ICJ genocide case against Israele (2024)
Stati Uniti Ambassador warned Sudafrica over alleged Russia arms transfers
Sudafrica joined BRICS and supports BRICS currency discussions

military.summary: No formal alliance. Stati Uniti and Sudafrica have historically cooperated on African peacekeeping through IMET and other programmes. However, Sudafrica's Russia-Cina military exercises and alleged arms transfers to Russia have severely damaged defence cooperation. The Stati Uniti has suspended some military assistance programmes. Engagement through ACOTA (Africa Contingency Operations Training) continues at a reduced level.
incidents: US Ambassador accused South Africa of secretly supplying arms to Russia (2023)
South Africa conducted joint naval exercises with Russia and China (2023)

financial.summary: The Stati Uniti is among Sudafrica's largest foreign investors, with significant exposure in financial services, mining, technology, and manufacturing. Sudafrica holds Stati Uniti Treasury bonds as FX reserves. Stati Uniti institutional investors hold Sudafrican equities and bonds. Investment has been dampened by Sudafrica's political risk and electricity crisis.

energy.summary: Stati Uniti energy firms have been involved in Sudafrica's energy sector, including gas exploration off the Southern Cape. The Stati Uniti has supported Sudafrica's Just Energy Transition Partnership (JETP) to assist coal phase-out. Growing Stati Uniti LNG interest in the Sudafrican market as Sudafrica diversifies away from coal.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific collaboration in health sciences (HIV/AIDS, TB, malaria research through PEPFAR and NIH), astronomy (Square Kilometre Array), and environmental sciences. The Stati Uniti-Sudafrica Fulbright programme remains active. Joint research through the Sudafrican-Stati Uniti Research Initiative (SAURI) and academic partnerships.
